trivia_bullets.text: Capture Sphere was first printed in Guilds of Ravnica (GRN, released October 5, 2018) as a blue common Aura in the Dimir/Azorius space, then reprinted in Ikoria: Lair of Behemoths (April 2020), Core Set 2021 (July 2020), Double Masters 2022 (July 2022), and Foundations Jumpstart (November 2024). It has appeared in six distinct sets across six years. [Scryfall prints search]

Artist Mark Behm illustrated the Guilds of Ravnica original and all subsequent core-set and supplemental printings. The Ikoria: Lair of Behemoths printing by Jakub Kasper is the only version with a different artist. Capture Sphere has no flavor text in any printing — the card relies entirely on its visual and mechanical concept to carry the imprisonment theme. [Scryfall prints search]

Capture Sphere functions as an instant-speed (flash) tap-lock Aura: the enchanted creature enters tapped immediately and never untaps. At {3}{U} it is costed similarly to other blue pacifism effects but the flash clause makes it a combat trick, allowing a defensive kill on an attacking creature. EDHREC rank 18,084 reflects light Commander play — it competes with hard removal that blue doesn't often have and soft taps that are usually cheaper. Legal in Pauper Commander. [Scryfall]

Community sentiment on Tappedout.net and cardsrealm.com: Capture Sphere is regarded as a solid Limited common — its flash turns it into an ambush tool — but underwhelming in Constructed where Aura-removal vulnerability makes it a poor investment of four mana. Players in Pauper prefer hard exile or bounce over tap-lock Auras. [cardsrealm.com card page via WebSearch snippet — sentiment]
sources: cardsrealm.com card page via WebSearch snippet
